homepage.heroHeadline: Expert Irrigation Solutions for Sacramento Homes
homepage.heroSubline: Specializing in systems built for Sacramento's extreme heat. We understand local soil challenges and water conservation needs.
aboutText: Founded in 2012, Legacy Irrigation began with a simple mission: to help Sacramento homeowners maintain lush landscapes despite our challenging climate. What started as a two-person operation now serves thousands across the valley.

We take a soil-first approach, designing systems that account for Sacramento's clay expansion and summer heat stress. Every installation includes water-saving technology to meet California's strict conservation standards.
As active members of the Sacramento Watershed Program, we're committed to responsible water use that preserves our region's natural beauty for future generations.

services.name: Drip Irrigation Installation
services.shortDescription: Precision watering systems that save water while keeping plants healthy in Sacramento's heat.
services.description:
In Sacramento's 100°F summers, traditional sprinklers waste water through evaporation. Our drip systems deliver water directly to plant roots with 90% efficiency.

We install commercial-grade tubing with pressure-compensating emitters that maintain consistent flow regardless of elevation changes common in East Sacramento properties. All systems include smart controllers that adjust watering based on real-time weather data from our local station network.

For older homes with clay soil, we add special drainage solutions to prevent water pooling during winter rains. Newer developments in Natomas benefit from our low-profile designs that preserve manicured landscaping.
process: Step 1: Soil analysis and plant water needs assessment
Step 2: Custom system design with water-saving calculations
Step 3: Installation with minimal landscape disruption
Step 4: Smart controller programming using local weather patterns
benefits: 50-70% water savings compared to sprinklers
Prevents leaf fungus common in Sacramento's morning humidity
Eliminates runoff on sloped properties
Compatible with rebates from SMUD Water Wise program

faq.question: How does drip irrigation handle Sacramento's clay soil?
faq.answer: We install deeper emitter lines with sand backfill to improve absorption in heavy clay. The slow drip rate prevents surface pooling while encouraging deep root growth that withstands summer heat.

faq.question: Can I retrofit my existing sprinkler system?
faq.answer: Yes, we frequently convert traditional systems in Midtown bungalows by capping sprinkler heads and adding drip lines. This preserves your existing piping while upgrading to water-efficient technology.

services.name: Smart Controller Upgrades
services.shortDescription: Weather-responsive irrigation technology that automatically adjusts for Sacramento's conditions.
services.description:
Sacramento's microclimates mean your irrigation needs change daily. Our smart controllers connect to local weather stations and soil sensors to water only when needed.

We install WiFi-enabled controllers that sync with Sacramento Municipal Utility District's Water Wise program, qualifying you for rebates. The system automatically skips watering after rainstorms (common November-March) and increases frequency during heat waves (June-September).

For historic districts like Land Park with mature trees, we add flow sensors that detect leaks before they damage root systems. Newer Elk Grove homes benefit from zone-specific programming that accounts for different sun exposure across properties.
process: Step 1: Evaluate current system zones and water usage
Step 2: Select controller model based on property size and plant types
Step 3: Professional installation with WiFi configuration
Step 4: Personalized training on mobile app features
benefits: Automatically adjusts for Sacramento's weather patterns
Prevents overwatering that leads to fungal issues
Remote control via smartphone app
Qualifies for $100+ SMUD rebates

faq.question: Will this work during Sacramento's power outages?
faq.answer: All controllers include battery backup that maintains programming for 72 hours. After major outages like those during fire season, the system performs a diagnostic check before resuming.

faq.question: How often do settings need manual adjustment?
faq.answer: The system self-adjusts seasonally, but we recommend checking settings during major transitions like our late May heat spikes or October cool-downs.

services.name: Sprinkler System Repair
services.shortDescription: Fast fixes for common Sacramento irrigation problems from cracked lines to misaligned heads.
services.description:
Sacramento's soil movement and hard water take a toll on irrigation systems. We specialize in diagnosing and repairing issues unique to our area.

For older neighborhoods with clay soil (like Curtis Park), we frequently fix pipes damaged by seasonal expansion. In newer developments with rocky fill dirt (such as North Natomas), we address emitter clogs from mineral buildup. Our mobile repair units carry parts for all major brands, with same-day service for emergencies during heat waves.

All repairs include a free system pressure test to identify other weak points. We also check controllers for proper programming - many Sacramento homes waste water by running systems at midday when evaporation is highest.
process: Step 1: Detailed inspection of all zones and components
Step 2: Identification of failed parts and underlying causes
Step 3: Repair using durable, locally-sourced replacements
Step 4: Pressure testing and efficiency evaluation
benefits: Same-day emergency service May-October
Free water audit with every repair
Parts guaranteed for Sacramento conditions
No overtime charges for weekend heat wave emergencies

faq.question: Why do my sprinkler heads keep breaking?
faq.answer: In Sacramento's hard water, mineral deposits can freeze in winter and crack plastic components. We install brass-reinforced heads that withstand temperature swings from 30°F winters to 115°F summers.

faq.question: How often should systems be inspected?
faq.answer: We recommend bi-annual checkups - before summer peak season and after fall's first rain to assess drainage performance.

services.name: Seasonal Maintenance
services.shortDescription: Proactive care to prepare irrigation systems for Sacramento's extreme seasons.
services.description:
Sacramento's dramatic seasonal shifts require different irrigation strategies. Our maintenance program protects your investment year-round.

Spring service includes pressurization checks before summer heat arrives, along with emitter cleaning to remove mineral deposits from our hard water. We adjust spray patterns to account for new plant growth in established gardens.

Fall preparation focuses on freeze protection - blowing out lines in elevated areas like Folsom where temperatures dip lower, while adding insulation to backflow preventers common in Roseville developments. We also reprogram controllers for reduced winter watering schedules per Sacramento County guidelines.
process: Step 1: Complete system inspection and pressure test
Step 2: Component cleaning and adjustments
Step 3: Seasonal reprogramming based on local forecasts
Step 4: Detailed report with efficiency metrics
benefits: Prevents costly emergency repairs during peak seasons
Maintains optimal water efficiency year-round
Extends system lifespan in harsh climate
Includes priority scheduling for urgent needs

faq.question: Why is fall maintenance important in Sacramento?
faq.answer: Our sudden temperature drops in November can crack pipes if water isn't properly drained. We also adjust schedules to prevent overwatering during rainy season when Sacramento gets most of its annual precipitation.

faq.question: Do you handle controller winterization?
faq.answer: Yes, we either weatherproof outdoor controllers or relocate them to garages - crucial for older systems in Land Park where freezing temps are more common than downtown.

services.name: New System Design
services.shortDescription: Custom irrigation solutions tailored to your property's specific Sacramento microclimate.
services.description:
Whether you're renovating a Midtown courtyard or landscaping a new Elk Grove home, we create irrigation systems designed for Sacramento's challenges.

Our designs begin with detailed soil analysis - particularly important in areas like East Sacramento where clay content varies block by block. We map sun exposure patterns to create watering zones that account for shade from mature valley oaks or intense afternoon sun on west-facing slopes.

All plans comply with Sacramento County's water ordinances while incorporating smart technology for future conservation requirements. For historic properties, we design discreet systems that preserve architectural integrity. New constructions benefit from our coordination with landscape architects to optimize both beauty and efficiency.
process: Step 1: Site survey including soil tests and solar mapping
Step 2: Water requirement calculations for all plant zones
Step 3: Custom design presentation with 3D modeling
Step 4: Installation by certified technicians
benefits: Tailored to your property's specific conditions
Future-proof technology that adapts to regulation changes
Integrated drainage solutions for heavy winter rains
Aesthetic designs that complement Sacramento architecture

faq.question: How long does new system installation take?
faq.answer: Most residential projects in Sacramento complete in 2-3 days. We minimize disruption by trenching strategically and cleaning daily - especially important in dense neighborhoods like McKinley Village.

faq.question: What's included in the design fee?
faq.answer: The flat-rate design covers all permits required by Sacramento County, engineering calculations, and three revision rounds to perfect your system layout.

reviews.name: Marisol G.
reviews.text: Our Midtown cottage's clay soil always caused drainage issues until Legacy installed a custom drip system. They added special sand channels that finally made our roses thrive despite 110°F summers. The team worked carefully around our historic brick walkways and even matched the tubing color to our mulch.

reviews.name: Devon K.
reviews.text: The Rachio controller Legacy installed saved us 40% on our water bill last summer. When that September heat wave hit, it automatically increased watering just enough to keep our Japanese maple alive without wasting water. Best part? Getting SMUD rebate paperwork handled for us.

reviews.name: Lien T.
reviews.text: Called at 7am when a broken line flooded our Rocklin backyard before a 105°F day. They had a crew out by 10am who not only fixed the PVC crack but found two other weak spots we didn't know about. No upcharge for emergency service - just honest pros who know Sacramento systems inside out.

reviews.name: Andre W.
reviews.text: Legacy designed an ingenious system for our odd-shaped Land Park lot with shady oaks and full-sun rose beds. The zoning accounts for how Sacramento's angle of sunlight changes seasonally. Two years later, everything grows perfectly with minimal water waste - even during drought restrictions.

reviews.name: Sophia R.
reviews.text: Their fall blowout service saved our pipes when that freak November freeze hit. While neighbors were repairing broken lines, ours worked perfectly. Now we religiously book their bi-annual checkups - worth every penny in Sacramento's extreme weather swings.

reviews.name: Carlos M.
reviews.text: Converting our 1980s sprinklers to drip was seamless. The crew pre-marked all utility lines (crucial in our older East Sac neighborhood) and finished a day early. Our water bill dropped 60% while the garden looks better than ever - even with mandatory conservation measures.

service-areas.title: Sacramento Area Irrigation Service Locations
service-areas.description: We serve Sacramento County including Elk Grove, Roseville and Folsom with local irrigation expertise.

serviceAreas.content: Legacy Irrigation specializes in providing efficient irrigation solutions for Elk Grove's newer master-planned communities. With summers regularly exceeding 100°F, proper irrigation is essential to maintain healthy landscapes while conserving water. Our smart irrigation systems are designed to adapt to Elk Grove's hot, dry climate, ensuring your lawn and plants thrive without wasting resources. We understand the unique needs of this growing community and offer tailored solutions for both residential and commercial properties.
serviceAreas.localContext: Elk Grove features newer developments (1990s-2020s) with master-planned communities and a hot, dry climate.

serviceAreas.content: Roseville's planned communities from the 1990s-2000s benefit from Legacy Irrigation's expertise in modern water management systems. The extreme summer heat demands irrigation systems that can deliver precise amounts of water without evaporation loss. We install and maintain drip irrigation, smart controllers, and water-efficient sprinkler systems perfect for Roseville's climate. Our services help homeowners and businesses maintain beautiful landscapes while complying with local water conservation regulations.
serviceAreas.localContext: Roseville consists of newer planned communities (1990s-2000s) with hot summers often above 100°F.

serviceAreas.content: Folsom's newer planned communities require irrigation systems that balance aesthetic appeal with water conservation. Legacy Irrigation designs systems that account for Folsom's hot, dry summers, incorporating features like weather-based controllers and high-efficiency nozzles. We work with homeowners associations and individual property owners to create sustainable irrigation solutions that preserve Folsom's beautiful landscapes while respecting the region's water resources. Our maintenance programs ensure systems operate at peak efficiency year-round.
serviceAreas.localContext: Folsom features newer planned communities with a climate marked by hot, dry summers.

serviceAreas.content: As Sacramento's trusted irrigation specialists, Legacy Irrigation helps property owners navigate the challenges of the region's Mediterranean climate. We design systems that account for both the intense summer heat and occasional winter freezes to protect your investment. From historic neighborhoods to new developments, our team understands Sacramento's diverse irrigation needs. We offer water-saving solutions that meet city regulations while keeping landscapes lush throughout the dry season.
serviceAreas.localContext: Sacramento features a mix of historic and modern properties with hot, dry summers and mild winters.

serviceAreas.content: Legacy Irrigation serves Rocklin's growing community with customized irrigation solutions for its newer developments. We understand the local soil conditions and climate challenges, designing systems that prevent runoff while ensuring proper coverage. Our water-efficient sprinkler systems and smart controllers help Rocklin homeowners maintain beautiful yards despite the summer heat. We also specialize in irrigation system winterization to protect against occasional freezing temperatures.
serviceAreas.localContext: Rocklin features newer developments with hot summers and needs efficient water management solutions.

serviceAreas.content: El Dorado Hills' upscale properties deserve irrigation systems that match their quality. Legacy Irrigation designs high-performance systems for the area's rolling terrain and microclimates. Our solutions account for the hotter valley temperatures and cooler hilltop conditions, ensuring proper water distribution throughout each property. We specialize in premium irrigation components that offer reliability and water efficiency for El Dorado Hills' discerning homeowners.
serviceAreas.localContext: El Dorado Hills features upscale properties with varied terrain and microclimates.

serviceAreas.content: Legacy Irrigation helps Lincoln residents maintain beautiful landscapes despite the region's hot, dry summers. We offer complete irrigation services from design to maintenance, ensuring optimal performance throughout the growing season. Our team stays current with Lincoln's water regulations to provide compliant, efficient solutions.
